source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, $PA$ and $PB$ are tangents to the circle $O$, with $A$ and $B$ being the points of tangency. Point $C$ is on the minor arc $\overset{\frown}{AB}$. If $\angle ACB = 110^\circ$, what is the measure of $\angle P$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} \textit{Draw OA, OB,\\
since PA, PB are tangents to the circle $\odot O$,\\
it follows that $\angle OAP = \angle OBP = 90^\circ$,\\
since $\angle ACB = 110^\circ$,\\
the central angle corresponding to the major arc AB is $220^\circ$,\\
hence, $\angle AOB = 360^\circ - 220^\circ = 140^\circ$,\\
therefore, $\angle P = 180^\circ - \angle AOB = \boxed{40^\circ}$,}

problem requires combining circle, arc, and tangent facts rather than a single direct lookup

answer_text provides a rule-based derivation that can be checked step by step
